context: Tests whether the TabletopKit skill preserves boundaries between TabletopKit, RealityKit, Group Activities, and GameKit.

checklist.name: TabletopKit authority
checklist.description: Makes TabletopKit the authority for table layout, equipment state, player seats, turns, and game actions.

checklist.name: RealityKit rendering
checklist.description: Uses RealityKit, EntityEquipment, or render delegates for 3D presentation and updates without making RealityKit transforms the source of synchronized game truth.

checklist.name: Group Activities sync
checklist.description: Uses Group Activities/SharePlay for synchronous multiplayer coordination and mentions required capability/session wiring at an appropriate level.

checklist.name: GameKit boundary
checklist.description: Treats GameKit/Game Center as optional identity, matchmaking, achievements, or leaderboards rather than the primary TabletopKit game-state synchronization mechanism.

checklist.name: Simulator caveat
checklist.description: Notes that simulator testing is useful for single-player layout but not a substitute for FaceTime/Group Activities multiplayer testing.

checklist.name: Observer and render flow
checklist.description: Describes observing confirmed TabletopKit actions and updating rendered entities through delegates or equivalent render callbacks.

checklist.name: Interaction availability
checklist.description: Uses current interaction configuration guidance, including availability gating TabletopInteraction.Configuration to visionOS 2.2+ and avoiding deprecated interaction APIs.

checklist.name: Dice and cards
checklist.description: Mentions dice/card state or toss helpers with correct availability when recommending onTossStart, TossOutcome, or face(for:).

checklist.name: No sibling overreach
checklist.description: Does not turn the answer into a general SwiftUI navigation, ARKit, SceneKit, or GameKit-only design that bypasses TabletopKit.

checklist.name: Review checklist
checklist.description: Ends with a practical checklist for validating state authority, rendering, multiplayer, and availability boundaries.
